executive_summary: Today, OFAC added 35 new entities and individuals to the SDN list under the IRAN-EO13902 program, primarily targeting Iranian companies and individuals involved in various sectors. The majority of the additions are entities based in Iran, with a few located in the United Kingdom, China, and the United Arab Emirates. No entities were removed or updated.

notable_entities.name: Shuqun LTD
notable_entities.significance: First entity listed, indicating a potential trend in targeting Iranian-related companies in the UK

notable_entities.name: Bakhshi Morteza Ali
notable_entities.significance: Individual listed with multiple aliases and identification numbers, suggesting potential for evasion and deception

notable_entities.name: Nooseb Trade Limited
notable_entities.significance: Entity based in China, highlighting the global reach of Iranian-related sanctions

risk_implications.description: The large number of additions to the SDN list under the IRAN-EO13902 program indicates a significant escalation of sanctions against Iranian entities and individuals, posing a high risk to companies involved in trade and finance with Iran
The inclusion of entities based in countries like the UK, China, and the UAE may disrupt global supply chains and pose a medium risk to companies with international operations
No cryptocurrency-related sanctions were imposed, indicating a low risk to cryptocurrency markets and transactions

program_highlights.note: All 35 additions were made under this program, targeting Iranian entities and individuals involved in various sectors

geographic_hotspots.activity: Sanctions targeting Iranian entities and individuals
Sanctions targeting entities based in China
Sanctions targeting entities based in the UK

compliance_recommendations: Conduct thorough due diligence on all business partners and transactions involving Iranian entities or individuals
Monitor sanctions lists and update internal watchlists regularly
Implement effective screening and reporting procedures to detect and prevent potential sanctions evasion
